title: Castle in the Air - Chapter Six: Which shows how Abdullah went from the frying pan into the fire (Fragment 2)
text:
Abdullah gave up and leaned against the wall, half dozing, half despairing. It must now be the heat of the day, when most folk in Zanzib took at least a short rest. Abdullah himself, when he was not visiting one of the public parks, usually sat on a pile of his less good carpets in the shade in front of his stall, drinking fruit juice, or wine if he could afford it, and chatting lazily with Jamal. No longer. And this is just my first day! he thought morbidly. I’m keeping track of the hours now. How long before I lose track even of days?

He shut his eyes. One good thing. A house-to-house search for the Sultan’s daughter would cause at least some annoyance to Fatima, Hakim, and Assif simply because they were known to be the only family Abdullah had. He hoped soldiers turned the purple emporium upside down. He hoped they slit the walls and unrolled all the carpets. He hoped they arrested—

Something landed on the floor beyond Abdullah’s feet.

So they throw me some food, Abdullah thought, and I would rather starve. He opened his eyes lazily. They shot wide of their own accord.

There, on the dungeon floor, lay the magic carpet. Upon it, peacefully sleeping, lay Jamal’s bad-tempered dog.

Abdullah stared at both of them. He could imagine how, in the heat of midday, the dog might lie down in the shade of Abdullah’s booth. He could see that it would lie on the carpet because it was comfortable. But how a dog—a dog !—could chance to say the command word was beyond him to understand entirely. As he stared, the dog began dreaming. Its paws worked. Its snout wrinkled, and it snuffled, as if it had caught the most delicious possible scent, and it uttered a faint whimper, as if whatever it smelled in the dream were escaping from it.

“Is it possible, my friend,” Abdullah said to it, “that you were dreaming of me and of the time I gave you most of my breakfast?”

The dog, in its sleep, heard him. It uttered a loud snore and woke up. Doglike, it wasted no time wondering how it came to be in this strange dungeon. It sniffed and smelled Abdullah. It sprang up with a delighted squeak, planted its paws among the chains on Abdullah’s chest, and enthusiastically licked his face.

Abdullah laughed and rolled his head to keep his nose out of the dog’s squiddy breath. He was quite as delighted as the dog was. “So you were dreaming of me!” he said. “My friend, I shall arrange for you to have a bowl of squid daily. You have saved my life and possibly Flower-in-the-Night’s, too!”

As soon as the dog’s rapture had abated a little, Abdullah began rolling and working himself along the floor in his chains, until he was lying, propped on one elbow, on top of the carpet. He gave a great sigh. Now he was safe. “Come along,” he said to the dog. “Get on the carpet, too.”

But the dog had found the scent of what was certainly a rat in the corner of the dungeon. It was pursuing the smell with excited snorts. At each snort Abdullah felt the carpet quiver beneath him. It gave him the answer he needed.

“Come along,” he said to the dog. “If I leave you here, they will find you when they come to feed me or question me, and they will assume I have turned myself into a dog. Then my fate will be yours. You have brought me the carpet and revealed me its secret, and I cannot see you stuck on a forty-foot stake.”

meta.author: Diana Wynne Jones
meta.sectionTitle: Chapter Six: Which shows how Abdullah went from the frying pan into the fire

vocabulary.definition: To become less intense or widespread.
vocabulary.example: As soon as the dog’s rapture had abated a little...

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of being slightly angry or irritated.
vocabulary.example: A house-to-house search for the Sultan’s daughter would cause at least some annoyance to Fatima, Hakim, and Assif.

vocabulary.definition: To seize someone by legal authority and take into custody.
vocabulary.example: He hoped they arrested—

vocabulary.definition: Easily annoyed or made angry.
vocabulary.example: Upon it, peacefully sleeping, lay Jamal’s bad-tempered dog.

vocabulary.definition: A small enclosed space where a person can sit or work.
vocabulary.example: He could imagine how... the dog might lie down in the shade of Abdullah’s booth.

vocabulary.definition: A natural combination of words that are frequently found together.
vocabulary.example: (General knowledge)

vocabulary.definition: Feeling or showing the loss of all hope.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah gave up and leaned against the wall, half dozing, half despairing.

vocabulary.definition: A dark prison or cell, typically underground.
vocabulary.example: There, on the dungeon floor, lay the magic carpet.

vocabulary.definition: A large retail store or place where many things are sold.
vocabulary.example: He hoped soldiers turned the purple emporium upside down.

vocabulary.definition: People in general; members of a community or group.
vocabulary.example: It must now be the heat of the day, when most folk in Zanzib took at least a short rest.

vocabulary.definition: To stop trying to do something because it is too difficult.
vocabulary.example: Abdullah gave up and leaned against the wall.

vocabulary.definition: To monitor or maintain awareness of something over time.
vocabulary.example: I’m keeping track of the hours now.

vocabulary.definition: To suddenly attack or criticize someone or something forcefully.
vocabulary.example: (General knowledge)

vocabulary.definition: In a way that is unhealthy or related to unpleasant subjects like death or disease.
vocabulary.example: No longer. And this is just my first day! he thought morbidly.

vocabulary.definition: A phrase composed of two or more words used as a single unit of meaning.
vocabulary.example: (General knowledge)

vocabulary.definition: To follow or chase with intent to catch or achieve something.
vocabulary.example: It was pursuing the smell with excited snorts.

vocabulary.definition: To tremble or shake with a slight rapid motion.
vocabulary.example: At each snort Abdullah felt the carpet quiver beneath him.

vocabulary.definition: A feeling of intense pleasure or joy.
vocabulary.example: As soon as the dog’s rapture had abated a little, Abdullah began rolling and working himself along the floor.

vocabulary.definition: An involuntary loud breathing sound made while asleep.
vocabulary.example: It uttered a loud snore and woke up.

vocabulary.definition: The projecting nose and mouth of an animal, especially a mammal.
vocabulary.example: Its snout wrinkled, and it snuffled, as if it had caught the most delicious possible scent.

vocabulary.definition: To breathe noisily through the nose, like an animal sniffing.
vocabulary.example: ...and it snuffled, as if it had caught the most delicious possible scent.

vocabulary.definition: A short, high-pitched sound.
vocabulary.example: It sprang up with a delighted squeak.

vocabulary.definition: A small stand or booth where goods are sold.
vocabulary.example: Usually sat on a pile of his less good carpets in the shade in front of his stall.

vocabulary.definition: To transform or change into something else.
vocabulary.example: They will assume I have turned myself into a dog.

vocabulary.definition: To say or make a sound.
vocabulary.example: The dog, in its sleep, heard him. It uttered a loud snore and woke up.

vocabulary.definition: To make a low, weak, broken sound expressing fear or pain.
vocabulary.example: It uttered a faint whimper, as if whatever it smelled in the dream were escaping from it.
